answered.question: Ruby + Rails support matrix for the gem
answered.answer: Ruby >= 3.2; CI matrix Rails 7.1, 7.2, 8.0; gemspec required_ruby_version = >= 3.2; Rails dependency >= 7.1 with no upper bound

answered.question: Where should the failsafe rescue live?
answered.answer: Per-subscriber rescue inside Subscribers::Base#deliver; on StandardError log via Rails.logger.error with subscriber name; re-raise in dev/test unless config.swallow_subscriber_errors = true

answered.question: Test framework for the gem's own test suite
answered.answer: Minitest (Rails-core convention; user has tdd-cycle skill calibrated for Minitest + fixtures; gem still ships matchers for both RSpec and Minitest for consumers)

answered.question: How should untyped events be detected so the linter rake task can report them?
answered.answer: Logger subscriber writes to Rails.logger AND appends JSONL to tmp/track_relay_untyped.jsonl when config.untyped_log_path or config.capture_untyped is set. JSONL records event name and param-name list only (no values). rake track_relay:lint reads the JSONL and dedupes by event+param signature.

answered.question: How should events pick which GA4 subscriber(s) fire?
answered.answer: Subscriber-side filters. TrackRelay.subscribe accepts only:/except: lists; default fires for every event. Filter runs inside Subscribers::Base#deliver before the per-subscriber rescue boundary. Catalog DSL stays vendor-neutral (no send_to: key); preserves one-catalog/many-destinations core value and keeps Phase 4 v2 vendors wireable without DSL changes.

answered.question: How is client_id resolved (cookie chain, parsing, fallback)?
answered.answer: Configurable resolver array config.client_id_resolvers = [Ga, AhoyVisitor, Session]; first non-nil wins; resolved once per request, memoized in TrackRelay::Current.client_id. _ga cookie parsed as last two dot-segments (e.g. GA1.2.123456789.1700000000 -> '123456789.1700000000'); malformed -> nil, chain continues. Session fallback = lazy UUID in session[:track_relay_client_id], stable across Rails session, no new top-level cookie.

answered.question: Where should GA4 constraints (REQ-10) be enforced?
answered.answer: Split by concern. Boot-time (Catalog DSL) validates event names: snake_case, <=40 chars, reserved-name refusal (frozen constant sourced from Google's GA4 reserved-events docs). Always raises; catalog refuses to load on violation. Call-time (Ga4MeasurementProtocol subscriber, before delivery) validates payload: <=25 custom params actually sent, param-name shape; follows REQ-05 (raise dev/test, log prod). Linter rake task extends untyped-events scan to flag GA4 violations on untyped events.

answered.question: How much detail belongs in track_relay_catalog.json?
answered.answer: Full typed schema mirroring the catalog DSL: { version, generated_at, events: { name: { params: { key: type }, required: [keys] } } }. Types are REQ-01's set (integer/string/float/boolean/datetime). JS validator follows REQ-05 (throw dev, console.warn+drop prod, configurable). Cache-busting via Sprockets fingerprint (Railtie hooks assets:precompile); JS loads /assets/track_relay_catalog-<hash>.json. Dev: file watcher on config/track_relay/**/*.rb re-runs manifest task. TypeScript types generation deferred to Phase 4.

inferred.text: Gem own test suite uses Minitest with ActiveSupport::TestCase for Rails-integration tests; no rspec/spec_helper
tmp/track_relay_untyped.jsonl is the canonical untyped-event sink; param values are never written, only param names
Subscribers::Base#deliver rescues StandardError and continues dispatch; re-raises in dev/test unless config.swallow_subscriber_errors is true
gemspec required_ruby_version >= 3.2; CI matrix runs Rails 7.1, 7.2, 8.0

TrackRelay.subscribe accepts only:/except: event-name filters; filter is evaluated in Subscribers::Base#deliver before the per-subscriber rescue boundary; absence of filter = fires for every event. Catalog DSL never carries vendor-routing keys.

config.client_id_resolvers is an ordered array of callables ([Ga, AhoyVisitor, Session] by default). First non-nil wins; result memoized in TrackRelay::Current.client_id per request. Default Ga resolver parses _ga as last two dot-segments; default Session resolver lazy-UUIDs into session[:track_relay_client_id].

Catalog boot validates event names against snake_case, <=40 chars, and a frozen GA4 reserved-name constant; violations raise unconditionally (catalog refuses to load). Payload-shape rules (<=25 params, param-name format) enforced inside Ga4MeasurementProtocol#deliver per REQ-05.

public/track_relay_catalog.json contains a typed schema per event: { params: { key: type }, required: [keys] } using REQ-01's type set, plus top-level version + generated_at. JS client (@track_relay/client) validates against this manifest before dispatching to gtag, mirroring REQ-05 dev-vs-prod semantics.

deferred.idea: Privacy/GDPR built-ins (IP masking, DNT, consent gating)
Custom Rubocop cop flagging raw gtag/ahoy.track calls
